example: The astronomer used photometry to determine the luminosity of distant stars by measuring their light intensity.
The pilot checked the altimeter before takeoff to ensure an accurate reading of the aircraft's altitude.
The meteorologist used an anemometer to measure the wind speed during the weather report.
The circuit was designed to handle a maximum current of 10 amperes to ensure safety.
Barometer readings are a useful indicator of how weather might differ at higher elevations compared to sea level.
The jeweler confirmed that the necklace was made of 18 karat gold, which is 75% pure gold mixed with other metals for added strength.
The geometry student calculated the perimeter of the rectangular garden to determine how much fencing would be needed.
Hypsometry helps map mountains and valleys accurately.
Hydrometry helps determine river flow rates for flood forecasting.
Chemists use spectrometry to identify unknown compounds in samples.
A voltmeter indicated a low battery charge in the flashlight.
A manometer indicated low tire pressure in the car.
The oven uses a thermocouple to regulate its temperature.
Metrology laboratories test and certify measuring instruments.
Calorimetry measures the heat released or absorbed during a chemical reaction.
Scientists use gravimetry to study the movement of tectonic plates.
The luminance unit measures the brightness of a television screen.
Densitometry revealed the concentration of pollutants in the river water.
